The substantive prose claims cannot be checked against the available evidence: the co-credit 'and Dawn Upshaw' (MB credits only Maria Schneider) and the two 2014 Grammy wins (Best Classical Contemporary Composition; Best Engineered Album, Classical) — neither appears in MB and there is no wiki. Not contradicted, just unverifiable.

album:alloy.notes: Embedded-MBID release (Tyshawn Sorey, Pi Recordings, 2014-10-27) matches exactly; all four keyTracks are the complete tracklist. Wiki stored_url is the Tyshawn Sorey artist page (correct entity), which itself states 'The first of these, Alloy, was released on Pi Recordings in 2014', confirming label and year.

album:conversations-ii.notes: Embedded-MBID release credits Roscoe Mitchell, Craig Taborn, Kikanju Baku (Wide Hive, 2014-04-15); all keyTracks present. Wiki stored_url is the matching album page; albumDNA ('recorded in 2013 and released on Wide Hive') is verbatim from it.

album:is-that-so.notes: Three-way collision. (1) MB embedded release 'Is That So?' (Stunt, 2014) is credited to Niels Vincentz, not Tom Harrell — its tracklist (Jericho, Haiku, Hot House, Cecilie, Modal Dancer) is exactly our keyTracks, so keyTracks 'match' the release but the release artist is not ours. (2) The albumDNA is wholesale about Tom Harrell's 'Infinity' (2019, HighNote), quoted verbatim from the stored wiki page 'Infinity (Tom Harrell album)' — a different album. (3) The stored wikipediaUrl points to that wrong Infinity page. Verdict entity_mismatch because the prose's factual skeleton belongs to a different entity; a repair-writer must rewrite the DNA for the actual 2014 'Is That So?' release and confirm the correct artist credit.

album:legacy.notes: Embedded-MBID release credits Gael Horellou + Abraham Burton (2014-02-11, DE, no label in MB); all five keyTracks (Clifford Jordan, Jackie McLean, Berchida's Song, Saint Leu, Palace Specia) are the complete tracklist. Abraham Burton is a genuine co-credit, so naming him is correct. Label field 'Unknown' cannot be contradicted (MB lists no label). Wiki resolution was opensearch and landed on the 'Legacy' disambiguation page (not our album) — discarded for fact-checking; ours.wikipediaUrl is null so wikiLinkVerdict is none_stored. albumDNA is editorial plus the two verified track names.

album:no-parking-tow-away-zone.notes: The embedded MBID resolved to the wrong release entirely: MB returns 'No Parking' by 'Origami Button' (2021-03-26, [no label], tracks Rot/Transient/Helix/Feel/Peach/Nervous/Stag) — different title, artist, year and tracklist from our 'No Parking Tow Away Zone' by Kenny Garrett (2014). None of our keyTracks (Old Folks, Wanderlust, True Blue, Airegin, Signs and Wonders) appear. So the MB document fails entity-matching and is discarded. Wikipedia opensearch returned nothing. No evidence survives to verify or refute anything; the embedded MBID is broken and needs re-resolution.

album:pages-from-an-imaginary-life.notes: Embedded-MBID release (Andy Bey, HighNote Records Inc., 2014) matches; all five keyTracks present. The producer claim 'Produced by DJ Premier collaborator Jose James' is unverifiable (no producer credit in MB). Wikipedia opensearch landed on the 'Rising Sun' disambiguation page (different title) — discarded; ours.wikipediaUrl null.

wrongClaims.problem: The albumDNA describes a different Chick Corea album ('Trilogy', a 2013 three-disc live trio album), not our item 'Solo Piano - Portraits' (2014, Concord Jazz, solo piano). The MB release for our item is the solo-piano album with no McBride/Blade and a solo tracklist.
wrongClaims.evidenceQuote: Trilogy is a 2013 live album by Chick Corea and his trio, featuring Corea on piano with Christian McBride on double bass and Brian Blade on drums.

album:solo-piano-portraits.notes: The catalog fields are correct — MB embedded release 'Chick Corea Solo Piano - Portraits' / release-group 'Solo Piano - Portraits' (Chick Corea, Concord Jazz, 2014) matches title/artist/year/label. But the albumDNA is wholesale about 'Trilogy' (different album), quoted verbatim from the stored wiki page 'Trilogy (Chick Corea album)', and the stored wikipediaUrl points to that wrong Trilogy page. Also keyTracks: 'Improv #1 / How Deep Is the Ocean?' and 'Waltz for Debby' are in the MB tracklist, but the three 'Chick Talks:' spoken-intro keyTracks are not. Verdict entity_mismatch driven by the prose describing a different entity; a repair-writer must rewrite the DNA for the actual Solo Piano - Portraits release and fix the wiki link.

album:sophisticated-swing.notes: Reissue case: our year 2014 matches the MB Cheesecake Records reissue (search match, topArtistSimilarity 1.0); the underlying album is Cannonball Adderley's 1957 EmArcy LP. All five keyTracks present in the MB tracklist. Wiki stored_url is the matching Sophisticated Swing album page; the entire albumDNA (fifth album, fourth on EmArcy, Nat Adderley/Junior Mance/Sam Jones/Jimmy Cobb, Chuck Stewart cover photo at the Ulysses S. Grant Monument) is verbatim from it. The 1957-vs-2014 gap is a reissue, not an error.

album:afrodeezia.notes: Embedded-MBID release (Marcus Miller, Blue Note, 2015-03-16) matches; all five keyTracks present. Wiki stored_url is the matching album page; albumDNA ('studio album by American bass-guitarist Marcus Miller ... released on March 16, 2015 by Blue Note Records. This is his debut release for Blue Note.') is verbatim from it.

album:in-out-and-around.notes: Album is correctly identified: MB embedded release 'In Out and Around' (Timeless Records, 2015-06-17) with all five keyTracks present. MB credits 'Mike Nock Quartet, Michael Brecker, George Mraz, Al Foster' — i.e. this is a Mike Nock-led date featuring Brecker; our catalog/DNA frame it as a Michael Brecker album. Brecker is a genuine credited performer, so the per-spec leniency on leader/sideman naming applies (not flagged), but a reviewer should note the leader is Mike Nock. The Brecker biography (15 Grammys, DownBeat Hall of Fame 2007, Berklee honorary doctorate 2004) is verbatim from the entity-matched Michael Brecker wiki page and concerns the right person, so it is verified, not unverifiable.

album:the-epic.notes: Core album facts are confirmed and clean: MB search match (topArtistSimilarity 1.0) for Kamasi Washington, Brainfeeder, 2015-05-05; all four keyTracks present; and 'third studio album ... first to be released on a record label ... released on May 5, 2015, by the Brainfeeder record label' is verbatim from the matching album wiki. The only uncheckable substantive claim is 'Brought jazz to a new audience through collaborations with Kendrick Lamar and Flying Lotus' — those named collaborations are not in the pack evidence (album wiki does not mention them; MB has none). Not contradicted, so unverifiable rather than a flag; the remaining 'sparked renewed interest in spiritual jazz' is editorial.

album:the-syndicate-live-at-the-hopbine-.notes: Embedded-MBID release matches exactly: Tubby Hayes, Gearbox Records, 2015-08-14, with all four keyTracks the complete tracklist. 2015 is the archival-release date of 1968 live material (the title itself states 1968), so no anachronism. Wiki stored_url is the Tubby Hayes artist page (correct entity); the sentence 'He is widely considered to be one of the finest jazz saxophonists to have emerged from Britain' is verbatim from it.

album:arclight.notes: Embedded-MBID release (Julian Lage, Mack Avenue Records, 2016-03-11) matches; all five keyTracks present. Wiki stored_url is the matching album page; albumDNA ('fourth solo album by American jazz guitarist Julian Lage, released by Mack Avenue Records on March 11, 2016. Two tracks were given a pre-release: "Nocturne" and "Harlem Blues".') is verbatim from it.

album:everythings-beautiful.notes: Embedded-MBID release matches (title, 2016-05-27, Blue Note among labels, credited artists Miles Davis + Robert Glasper). All 5 keyTracks present in MB tracklist. Prose's only biographical claim 'won five Grammy Awards from 11 nominations' is verbatim in the Robert Glasper wiki extract. The album is jointly credited to Miles Davis (a remix/reimagining of Davis material); our prose credits only Glasper, who is a genuine co-credited artist, so this is acceptable editorial framing, not a mismatch. Stored wikiUrl is the Glasper artist page (correct artist) though not an album-specific page.

album:in-movement.notes: Embedded-MBID release matches (title, 2016-05-06, ECM Records, credited artists Jack DeJohnette + Ravi Coltrane + Matthew Garrison). All 5 keyTracks present in MB tracklist. Bio sentence 'Co-owner of the record label RKM Music, he has produced music for pianist Luis Perdomo, guitarist David Gilmore, and trumpeter Ralph Alessi' is verbatim from the Ravi Coltrane wiki extract. Album is a co-led DeJohnette/Coltrane/Garrison trio; prose foregrounds Coltrane, who is a genuine co-credit.

album:inner-spectrum-of-variables.notes: MB search match (sim 1.0) to 'The Inner Spectrum of Variables' (our title drops the leading 'The'), 2016-06-03, Pi Recordings, Tyshawn Sorey. All 3 keyTracks present. Entity is clearly correct. No wikipedia. The substantive biographical claim 'earned him a MacArthur Fellowship' cannot be verified against the pack evidence (no wiki); not contradicted. 'triple album' also not confirmable from MB (MB lists 6 tracks but that does not preclude a 3-disc set). Rest of prose is editorial characterization.

album:live-under-the-sky-tokyo-84.notes: Embedded-MBID release matches (title, 2016, Hi-Hat, status Bootleg, credited artists Gil Evans + Jaco Pastorius). All 5 keyTracks present in MB tracklist. The albumDNA is actually Gil Evans artist-bio prose copied verbatim from the Gil Evans wiki extract ('Ian Ernest Gilmore Evans was a Canadian-American jazz pianist... best known for his acclaimed collaborations with Miles Davis') rather than album-specific content, but Gil Evans is a genuine credited artist on this release and every bio claim matches the wiki, so no factual problem. Repair note: prose describes the artist, not this live/bootleg album.

album:live-under-the-sky-tokyo-84-pastor.notes: Same embedded-MBID release as the Gil Evans entry (credited Gil Evans + Jaco Pastorius, 2016, Hi-Hat, Bootleg). Jaco Pastorius is a genuine co-credit. All 5 keyTracks present in MB tracklist. Prose is thin; 'big band recording' is a defensible characterization of the Gil Evans Orchestra context and is not contradicted by MB. No wikipedia. Everything checkable is consistent.

album:the-mezzo-sax-encounter.notes: Embedded-MBID release matches (title, 2016, Cowbell Music, credited artists Benjamin Koppel + Joe Lovano + Kenny Werner + Scott Colley + Johnathan Blake). All 5 keyTracks present in MB tracklist. Bio claim 'earned a Grammy Award and several mentions in Down Beat magazine's critics' & readers' polls' is verbatim from the Joe Lovano wiki extract. Lovano is a genuine co-credit; prose foregrounds him.

album:liberation-narratives.notes: The embedded MBID resolved to the WRONG release: MB returns 'Narratives' (2013) by Nathaniel Johnstone with an unrelated tracklist (Gepetto's Lament, Baba Yaga, ...), NOT Nicole Mitchell's 'Liberation Narratives'. album:your-queen-is-a-reptile.notes: MB search match (sim 1.0): 'Your Queen Is a Reptile', 2018-03-30, impulse!, Sons of Kemet. All 4 keyTracks present in the MB tracklist (Ada Eastman, Angela Davis, Harriet Tubman, Doreen Lawrence), and the album-defining claim that each track is named after a Black woman is directly supported by the tracklist (every title is 'My Queen Is [name]').
